articles.title: Family Entertainment Group Names Nick DiMatteo SVP of Equipment Operations & Strategy
articles.summary: FEG, one of the largest FEC operators in the US, has appointed industry veteran Nick DiMatteo, ICAE, as Senior Vice President of Equipment Operations & Strategy. DiMatteo brings over 25 years of amusement industry experience to the role. The hire signals FEG's focus on optimizing its equipment portfolio and operational strategy across its portfolio of venues.

articles.title: Family Entertainment Group Appoints Jason Clark as Chief Financial Officer
articles.summary: FEG has brought on Jason Clark as CFO, effective May 26, 2026, as the company enters a period of significant organizational growth. Clark's background spans restaurant, hospitality, and entertainment finance. The dual executive appointments at FEG suggest the company is positioning for expansion or significant capital activity.

articles.title: Private Equity Firm Periscope Invests in Amusement Connect
articles.summary: Periscope, a private equity firm, has made an investment in Amusement Connect, signaling growing investor interest in FEC technology and management platforms. The deal underscores the increasing appeal of the amusement and FEC sector to outside capital. FEC operators should watch how this investment shapes Amusement Connect's product development and market reach.

articles.title: Brogent Debuts 'R-Ride' 360-Degree Motion Theater at IAAPA Asia 2026
articles.summary: Brogent Technologies unveiled its R-Ride at IAAPA Expo Asia 2026, a motion theater featuring a 360-degree panoramic screen and next-generation ride systems. The company also announced strategic content partnerships with major Japanese IPs and revealed five new Southeast Asian projects. FEC operators considering flying theaters or immersive ride upgrades should take note of this new format option.

articles.title: Red Raion Releases 'Dragon's Gold - The Chase' for 5D, VR, Dome, and Flying Theatre Formats
articles.summary: CGI studio Red Raion has launched Dragon's Gold - The Chase, a new family-focused film available for licensing across multiple attraction formats including 5D, VR, dome, and flying theatre. The title is designed for high-throughput venues and emphasizes repeatability with synchronized effects and comedy. FEC operators running media-based attractions now have a new turnkey content option to refresh their lineup.

articles.title: Location-Based Entertainment Market Seeing Strong Global Growth
articles.summary: A new market report highlights booming worldwide growth in the location-based entertainment sector, encompassing FECs, immersive experiences, and out-of-home entertainment venues. The report points to rising consumer demand for experiential spending as a key driver. Operators and investors can use this data to support expansion decisions and capital pitches.

articles.title: WildBrain CPLG to Expand Miraculous Location-Based Entertainment Licensing Across EMEA and APAC
articles.summary: WildBrain CPLG has been appointed to manage location-based entertainment opportunities for the Miraculous brand across EMEA and APAC markets. The Miraculous franchise has a large and passionate family audience, making it an attractive IP for FECs, trampoline parks, and indoor play venues. Operators in those regions looking for licensable children's IP should explore partnership opportunities.

articles.title: New Entertainment District 'The Gallery' Set to Break Ground in Madison This Summer
articles.summary: A new mixed-use entertainment district called The Gallery is scheduled to break ground in Madison this summer, adding another large-scale entertainment development to the growing market. Projects like this reflect the continued investor appetite for destination entertainment complexes combining dining, gaming, and attractions. FEC operators should monitor how these entertainment districts affect local competitive dynamics.

articles.title: NRG Adventure Park Moving Forward at Jacksonville's Southside Commons
articles.summary: NRG Adventure Park is advancing its development plans at the Southside Commons mixed-use project in Jacksonville, Florida. The project represents another example of adventure park concepts anchoring retail and mixed-use developments. FEC operators and developers can look to this as a model for embedding entertainment venues within larger commercial projects.

articles.title: Urban Air Adventure Park Opens New Location in Salem, Oregon
articles.summary: Urban Air Adventure Park has opened a new location in Salem, Oregon, continuing the franchise's national expansion. The Salem venue adds to the growing footprint of the Urban Air brand across secondary markets. Operators in the Pacific Northwest should be aware of the increased competitive presence in the region.

articles.title: Inside Urban Air Adventure Park's New Salem, Oregon Location
articles.summary: A facility tour of Urban Air's new Salem, Oregon location highlights the attraction mix and layout typical of the franchise's current build-out standard. The venue showcases Urban Air's evolving attraction offering for mid-size markets. FEC operators can use this as a competitive benchmark when evaluating their own attraction mix.

articles.title: Slick City Action Park Opens at Potomac Mills in Northern Virginia
articles.summary: Slick City Action Park, a slide-based indoor attraction concept, has opened a new location at Potomac Mills mall in Northern Virginia. The opening continues Slick City's expansion into major suburban retail centers. FEC operators should note the brand's momentum as it competes for family entertainment spending in the Mid-Atlantic market.

articles.title: Northern Lights Recreation FEC Opens Adventure Golf & Sports-Designed Mini Golf Course
articles.summary: Northern Lights Recreation FEC in Harbor Springs, Michigan will hold a grand opening for its new Adventure Golf & Sports-designed mini golf course July 5-12, adding an outdoor anchor attraction to the venue's offerings. The course is featured within Rocky's Beer Garden, blending family entertainment with adult hospitality. The project is a useful case study for FECs considering adding outdoor mini golf to drive incremental visits.

articles.title: Tenpin Opens New Entertainment Centre in Blackpool
articles.summary: Tenpin, the UK bowling and entertainment chain, has opened a new venue in Blackpool, continuing its expansion across Britain. The opening adds bowling, arcades, and dining to a market that attracts significant tourist traffic. UK-based FEC operators should take note of Tenpin's continued growth in leisure destinations.

articles.title: Chuck E. Cheese Offers Free Adventure Zone Admission for International Day of Play
articles.summary: Chuck E. Cheese partnered with The Toy Foundation to offer free Adventure Zone admission on International Day of Play as part of a June fundraiser. The promotion is an example of cause-marketing tactics that drive foot traffic while building brand goodwill. FEC operators can draw inspiration from this promotional model for community engagement campaigns.

articles.title: Riot at Raleigh Urban Air Forces Early Closure; Three Women Charged
articles.summary: A disturbance at an Urban Air location in Raleigh, North Carolina escalated into a riot, forcing the venue to close early and resulting in three arrests. The incident raises important questions about crowd management, security staffing, and incident response protocols for FEC operators. Venues serving teens and young adults should review their security procedures and local law enforcement coordination in light of this event.

articles.title: London-Inspired Indoor Entertainment Venue with Interactive Soccer and Games Coming to Paris
articles.summary: A new indoor entertainment concept featuring virtual soccer, interactive games, foosball, cocktails, and fast food is set to open in Paris, imported from London. The format blends social gaming with food and beverage in a way that targets young adults and groups. FEC operators can look to this concept as a signal of the growing adult-oriented social entertainment trend expanding across Europe.

articles.title: Longtime Menasha, Wisconsin Bowling Alley Permanently Closes
articles.summary: A long-established bowling alley in Menasha, Wisconsin has permanently shut its doors, adding to a broader trend of standalone bowling centers struggling in the current market. The closure highlights the challenges facing single-attraction venues competing against multi-activity FECs. Operators of traditional bowling centers should take note as the industry continues to shift toward diversified entertainment offerings.

articles.title: Floyd Bowling & Amusement Center Thrives With Community-Focused Approach
articles.summary: Floyd Bowling & Amusement Center in Rome, Georgia is highlighted for its serious commitment to the sport of bowling alongside amusement offerings, demonstrating that a community-rooted strategy can sustain traditional bowling venues. The center's blend of league play, amusement games, and local loyalty offers a model for operators looking to maintain bowling as an anchor attraction. The feature is a useful counter-narrative to closure stories in the bowling sector.

articles.title: Bear River Casino's New Entertainment Zone Attracts Regional Business Interest
articles.summary: Chamber of commerce members from Fortuna and Eureka toured the new entertainment zone at Bear River Casino Resort, signaling regional interest in the venue as an economic driver. The entertainment zone appears to combine FEC-style attractions with the casino hospitality environment. This model of integrating entertainment attractions within casino resorts is a growing trend worth watching for FEC developers.

articles.title: Arizona's Top 10 Indoor Entertainment Attractions for 2026 Ranked
articles.summary: AZ Big Media has released its annual ranking of the top indoor entertainment attractions in Arizona for 2026, spotlighting venues competing for family leisure spending in the market. The list serves as a competitive landscape snapshot for FEC operators and investors evaluating the Arizona market. Understanding which concepts are capturing consumer attention can inform product and marketing decisions.

articles.title: Dollywood Extends Employee Appreciation Discount Program into Summer
articles.summary: Dollywood has extended its Public Employee Appreciation Days promotion through the summer, offering discounted admission to veterans, first responders, and teachers. The promotion has proven popular and represents an effective community relations and demand-generation tool. FEC operators can look to this model when designing group and promotional discount programs that drive off-peak traffic.

articles.title: Exhibition Hub Launches Immersive Tutankhamun Experience in Antwerp
articles.summary: Exhibition Hub and Semmel Exhibitions have opened Treasures of Tutankhamun: The Experience in Antwerp, using immersive technology and environmental storytelling to bring ancient history to life. The project demonstrates how IP-driven immersive experiences can attract broad family and cultural audiences. FEC operators adding immersive or pop-up experience attractions can study this format for content and design inspiration.

articles.title: Oregon Robotics Dealers Automate Business Operations Without Replacing Workers
articles.summary: New robotics dealers in Oregon are helping businesses automate operational tasks while maintaining their human workforce, according to a new report. The trend is relevant for FEC operators exploring automation for food service, ticketing, and facility management. Understanding how to deploy robotics as a complement to staff rather than a replacement may help operators address labor challenges without workforce disruption.

articles.title: Massachusetts Museums Deploy AI to Create Interactive Historical Experiences
articles.summary: Several Massachusetts museums are using artificial intelligence to create immersive, interactive encounters with historical figures and stories. The application of AI-driven character interaction is increasingly relevant to FEC operators developing escape rooms, immersive attractions, and educational entertainment offerings. This trend points toward affordable AI tools that smaller venues may soon be able to adopt.

articles.title: Why Top Theme Park Rides Do More Than Drive Attendance
articles.summary: An analysis explores how marquee attractions at theme parks generate value beyond raw attendance numbers, including merchandise revenue, media coverage, and brand equity. The insights are transferable to FEC operators considering capital investments in signature attractions. Understanding the full ROI of a flagship ride or experience can strengthen the business case for major capex decisions.

articles.title: The Financial Scale Behind the UK's Newest Theme Park
articles.summary: A breakdown of the major capital investment and projected economic impact behind the UK's newest theme park offers a useful benchmark for large-scale entertainment development. The figures illustrate the scale of funding, job creation, and visitor projections involved in greenfield park development. FEC investors and developers can use this data to contextualize capital requirements for new builds.

articles.title: Frostbite's Arcade & Creamery Relocates to Larger Fulton Market
articles.summary: Frostbite's Arcade & Creamery, a small-format FEC combining arcade games with ice cream, is relocating from Holts Summit to the larger Fulton, Missouri market. The move suggests the concept is gaining traction and seeking a bigger customer base. The arcade-plus-food-and-beverage model continues to prove viable as a small-footprint FEC format.
